channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Orioles to honor Key Bridge collapse victims on opening day
item.description: The Orioles will honor victims, families and first responders of the Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Rays' Wander Franco placed on administrative leave through June 1 as sexual abuse probe continues
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Tampa Bay All-Star shortstop Wander Franco was placed on administrative leave through June 1 under an agreement between Major League Baseball and the players' association while the investigation continues in an alleged relationship with a minor.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pirates' Paul Skenes to donate $100 to Gary Sinise Foundation for every strikeout this season
item.description: Pittsburgh Pirates pitching prospect Paul Skenes announced Wednesday he will donate $100 for every strikeout he throws to the Gary Sinise Foundation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Gatos athletic director Ken Perrotti recognized by his peers
item.description: Los Gatos High School graduate Ken Perrotti was named by the Central Coast Section Athletic Directors Association as the 2024 Athletic Director of the Year. Perrotti will be recognized at the California State Athletic Directors Association conference on April 10-14 in San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Phil Jensen, correspondent, The Mercury News

item.title: DeRozan, Vucevic lead Bulls past Pacers 125-99 to end 3-game losing streak
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — DeMar DeRozan scored 27 points and Nikola Vucevic added 22 with 12 rebounds to lead the Chicago Bulls past the Indiana Pacers 125-99 on Wednesday night to snap a three-game skid. Coby White had 18 points to help Chicago take the season series from Indiana 3-1.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: These three questions will determine SF Giants’ fate in 2024
item.description: Jung Hoo Lee, the health of the starting rotation and the viability of pitching and defense in the NL West will factor into Giants' success this season.
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: Super-sized March Madness stats in women's Sweet 16
item.description: The numbers have been stunning in this memorable season for women’s college basketball. Iowa’s Caitlin Clark has broken scoring records. UConn’s Paige Bueckers is producing eye-popping statistics again after missing most of the last two seasons with knee injuries.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian soccer club with several transgender players dominates women's tournament, sparking outrage
item.description: An Australian soccer club with apparently five transgender players won a women's preseason tournament, sparking outrage in the country and online.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oakland A’s season preview: 5 things to watch for Opening Day and beyond
item.description: The Oakland A’s will host the Cleveland Guardians at the Coliseum on Thursday evening, officially kicking off what could be a fascinating season. Fascinating off the field, at least.
item.dc:creator: Jason Mastrodonato, The Mercury News

item.title: Play ball! White Sox ready for home opener on the South Side as 2024 MLB season gets underway
item.description: CHICAGO — Even if summer weather isn't here yet, the Boys of Summer are back. Thursday is Opening Day on the South Side, as the Chicago White Sox embark on their 2024 campaign against the Detroit Tigers at Guaranteed Rate Field on the first full day of the Major League Baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: That time the Tigers played the World Series champs with replacement players
item.description: The Tigers facing the two-time defending World Series champions was a huge game that people wanted to see.
item.dc:creator: Dane Kelly, Local 4

item.title: The 'Sho' goes on; mural of Dodger slugger Shohei Ohtani unveiled ahead of home opener
item.description: As anticipation builds for the “Boys in Blue” this season, the excitement is also, well, on the building as a giant mural of Shohei Ohtani was unveiled today in Los Angeles’ Little Tokyo just a day ahead of the Dodgers home opener. Towering 11 stories over L.A.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: New Stanford basketball coach cries while talking about resources school can provide his special needs son
item.description: (KRON) -- New Stanford men's basketball coach Kyle Smith sees the school as his dream job -- not just for what could happen on the court, but also for how the university can help his family.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Officials admit to missed call on final play of Clippers win over 76ers
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Officials said they missed a foul call in the closing seconds of the Los Angeles Clippers' 108-107 win over Philadelphia on Wednesday night, denying the 76ers a possible victory in their fight for better playoff positioning.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN COONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Projecting the Chicago White Sox's Opening Day lineup
item.description: CHICAGO — It's that time of year again White Sox fans. We're less than 24 hours away from first pitch on Opening Day and the South Siders just announced their 26-man roster that will take the field against the Detroit Tigers for game No. 1.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: NHL's Capitals and NBA's Wizards are staying in Washington after Virginia arena deal collapses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Ted Leonsis told District of Columbia Mayor Muriel Bowser late last year that the NBA's Washington Wizards and NHL's Washington Capitals he owns would probably be leaving Washington for Virginia, she told him no, they would not. Ultimately, she proved to be right.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, SARAH RANKIN and MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Warriors' Draymond Green is ejected less than 4 minutes into game against Magic
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green was ejected less than four minutes into the Warriors’ game against the Orlando Magic on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Athlete of the Week: White Plains High School senior Logan McCormick
item.description: WHITE PLAINS, N.Y. (PIX11) -- The offseason. A chance to use what you learned on the court to be better for the next year. Few players take better advantage of the time than White Plains High School's Logan McCormick.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Source: LHP Parker Mushinski makes Astros opening day roster
item.description: Left-handed reliver Parker Mushinski has made the Astros opening day roster, a league source tells KPRC 2.
item.dc:creator: Ari Alexander, KPRC 2

item.title: White Sox announce Opening Day roster
item.description: CHICAGO — Following their workout Wednesday at Guaranteed Rate Field, the Chicago White Sox announced their Opening Day roster for the 2024 Major League Baseball regular season.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-Buccaneers tight end, Rob Gronkowski, visits veterans in Tampa
item.description: The Super Bowl Champion and former tight end for the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, Rob Gronkowski, paid a visit to veterans in Tampa on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bay FC sells out inaugural home game Saturday, traffic delays expected
item.description: (KRON) -- After winning its first match 1-0 on the road against Angel City FC, the Bay Area's new women's professional soccer team, Bay FC, will play its first home game Saturday. On Wednesday, the team announced that the match was sold out.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: New York Mets 2024 home opener postponed due to expected rain
item.description: FLUSHING, N.Y. (PIX11) -- The New York Mets 2024 home opener has been postponed due to rain expected in the forecast, the team announced on Wednesday. The game originally scheduled for Thursday against the Milwaukee Brewers will now happen on Friday at 1:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Rizk, PIX 11

item.title: The hottest tickets in baseball this season are in Los Angeles
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers made some major investments over the winter, committing more than $1 billion to a pair of Japanese superstars, but it appears it won't take long for the club to recover the costs.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Jasmin Paris becomes first woman to complete Barkley Marathons
item.description: After attempting the race twice before, Jasmin Paris became the first woman to finish the Barkley Marathons.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: CMU Basketball Head Coach Mike DeGeorge leaving for D1 job
item.description: One day after the RMAC's Player of the Year Trevor Baskin announced his decision to transfer, the RMAC's Coach of the Year Mike DeGeorge has accepted the head coaching job at Cal Poly - a D1 program in the Big West.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Aztec fans, including 'Show Grandmas,' gear up for Sweet 16
item.description: San Diego State Aztec fans are ready to continue cheering on their team into the Sweet 16 on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Alani Letang, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Padres home opener
item.description: After splitting the first two games of the Major League Baseball season against the Los Angeles Dodgers in Seoul, South Korea, the Padres get set for their home opener this week.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Ramirez hoping for successful "second chapter" with De La Hoya
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. - Avenal native Jose Ramirez will be returning to the ring in Fresno on April 27th at the Save Mart Center, hoping to begin a successful "second chapter" of his boxing career under the promotional umbrella of one of his childhood idols, boxing legend Oscar De La Hoya.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bemis, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Moose on the Loose: Shot in the arm
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Sometimes athletes surprise you with their performance, and Knicks guard Donte DiVincenzo has certainly done that. He was drafted in the first round, 17th overall, of the 2018 NBA Draft after helping the Villanova Wildcats win a national championship.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Group files lawsuit to stop Dodger Stadium gondola project
item.description: The proposal to build a skyway to link Dodger Stadium with Los Angeles Union Station and Chinatown faces another hurdle after a group of activists filed a lawsuit to have the project's environmental impact report tossed out.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Former Kansas City Chiefs cheerleader dies after childbirth complications
item.description: A former Kansas City Chiefs cheerleader has died after giving birth to her daughter, who also passed away, family members told Nexstar's WDAF.
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani says he has 'never' gambled on sports
item.description: The MLB star spoke publicly for the first time since allegations of theft and gambling opened an investigation into his interpreter.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: Bracket not busted: All top 8 seeds advance to Sweet 16 in chalky March Madness
item.description: March Madness arrived with visions of chaos. Based on last year's bracket, there was little reason to doubt it. The only surprise so far has been the lack of pandemonium. The top two seeds from each region are headed to the Sweet 16 for just the fifth time. One double-digit seed will join them.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: shortstop Ben Newton
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) –Fresno state senior shortstop Ben Newton has played a consistent role in the Bulldogs' lineup. Batting sixth he provides a spark when he's up to bat. Newton credits the Bulldogs' success this season to their offseason work and veteran group.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: Fresno State Pro Day
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) – 20 NFL scouts were in attendance at Fresno State's Pro Day last week. Eleven draft-eligible players took part in drills. Former Bulldog DB Carlton Johnson was the only Bulldog invited to the NFL Scouting combine. He injured his hamstring and was unable to participate.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: How March Madness hits close to home
item.description: The Colbran Calfe was rocking as De Beque native Brennan Rigsby and 11-seed Oregon pulled away from 6-seed South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
